action_templates.title: Send an Email to Lawmakers
action_templates.description: Use our email tool to contact your elected officials about a current climate policy, using a customizable template and your own talking points.

action_templates.title: Share a Climate Petition on Social Media
action_templates.description: Choose a high-impact climate petition and share it with your networks on at least two social platforms.

action_templates.title: Attend a Local Climate Rally
action_templates.description: Join a local march or rally demanding bold action on climate justice and environmental protection.

action_templates.title: Join a Neighborhood Climate Cleanup
action_templates.description: Participate in a community cleanup that reduces pollution and builds support for climate solutions where you live.

action_templates.title: Call Your Representative About Climate Justice
action_templates.description: Make a brief phone call to your representative urging them to support frontline climate justice communities.

action_templates.title: Set Up a Monthly Pledge for Economic Justice
action_templates.description: Commit to a recurring monthly donation to sustain long-term economic justice organizing.

action_templates.title: Share Your Student Debt Story
action_templates.description: Submit a short written or video testimony about how student debt affects your life to support debt cancellation campaigns.

action_templates.title: Attend a Police Accountability Town Hall
action_templates.description: Show up at a community forum to ask questions and push for local police reform.

action_templates.title: Volunteer at a Voter Registration Table
action_templates.description: Help community members check their registration status and sign up to vote at a local event.

action_templates.title: Join a Voting Rights Textbank
action_templates.description: Use an online tool to text voters with information about their rights and upcoming elections.

action_templates.title: Sign a Community Letter for Immigration Reform
action_templates.description: Add your name to a community letter calling for humane immigration and refugee policies.

action_templates.title: Attend a Tenant Organizing Meeting
action_templates.description: Meet with neighbors facing rising rents to coordinate a collective response.

action_templates.title: Sign a Petition for Stronger Rent Control
action_templates.description: Support local rent control efforts by signing and sharing a targeted petition.

action_templates.title: Read a Primer on Intersectional Organizing
action_templates.description: Learn the basics of intersectional organizing and how issues like race, class, and gender intersect in activism.

articles.title: Police Reform 101: Key Terms and Debates
articles.body:
This introductory article explains the core concepts behind police reform, including accountability, transparency, and community control. It breaks down common proposals such as body cameras, civilian review boards, and shifts in budget priorities.

We also explore how reform intersects with broader criminal justice movements, and why communities most impacted by police violence are leading the call for change.
articles.summary: An introduction to core concepts and proposals in the movement for police reform.

articles.title: Rethinking Traffic Stops: A Path Toward Police Reform
articles.body:
Traffic stops are one of the most common forms of police contact with civilians, and they disproportionately affect Black and Brown drivers. This article outlines alternative models for handling minor traffic violations, from unarmed traffic safety teams to automated reminders.

By shifting responsibility away from armed officers, cities can reduce the chance of violent encounters while still supporting road safety.
articles.summary: How changing the way we handle traffic enforcement can advance police reform.

articles.title: Why Data Transparency Matters for Police Reform
articles.body:
Without accurate data, communities cannot hold police departments accountable. Public reporting on use of force, stops, searches, and complaints is essential for understanding patterns of harm.

This article walks through examples of cities that have adopted strong transparency practices, and how organizers have used that information to win policy changes.
articles.summary: Exploring the role of open data and public reporting in effective police reform.

articles.title: Following the Money: A Deep Dive Into Police Budgets
articles.body:
Police budgets consume a large share of many city budgets. Understanding where that money goes is the first step toward shifting resources into community-based safety.

We examine sample city budgets, explain common line items, and highlight successful campaigns that have reallocated funds.
articles.summary: A longer explainer on how police budgets work and how communities are changing them.

articles.title: Community-Led Safety Models Beyond Traditional Police Reform
articles.body:
Some communities are moving beyond traditional police reform toward community-led safety models. These include crisis response teams, violence interrupters, and restorative justice circles.

We share case studies, lessons learned, and steps your city can take to pilot similar programs.
articles.summary: How community-led safety initiatives offer alternatives to traditional policing.

articles.title: Climate Justice on the Frontlines
articles.body: Frontline communities are already facing the worst harms of climate change. This article explores their leadership, demands, and strategies for winning climate justice.
articles.summary: An overview of frontline climate justice leadership and demands.

articles.title: A Brief History of Voting Rights in the United States
articles.body: From Reconstruction to the present, voting rights have expanded because people organized for change. This article traces key turning points in that history.
articles.summary: A short history of voting rights struggles and victories.

articles.title: Understanding Rent Burden and Housing Insecurity
articles.body: Millions of households spend more than 30% of their income on rent, leaving little room for emergencies or savings. This article explains how rent burden is measured and why it matters for housing policy.
articles.summary: How rent burden shapes housing insecurity and what can be done.

articles.title: The Case for Canceling Student Debt
articles.body: Student debt is a major driver of racial and economic inequality. We outline the moral, economic, and legal arguments for broad-based debt cancellation.
articles.summary: Why canceling student debt is essential for economic justice.

articles.title: Asylum 101: How the Process Really Works
articles.body: Asylum seekers face a complex and often hostile system. This article explains the basic steps in the asylum process and common misconceptions.
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to the U.S. asylum system.

articles.title: Renewable Energy Basics for Climate Action
articles.body: Transitioning to renewable energy is one of the fastest ways to cut climate pollution. Learn the basics of solar, wind, and energy efficiency.
articles.summary: An explainer on how renewable energy fits into climate solutions.

articles.title: Raising the Minimum Wage: Myths and Facts
articles.body: Debates about the minimum wage are full of misinformation. We address common myths and share what research actually shows.
articles.summary: Evidence-based answers to common questions about raising the minimum wage.

articles.title: Getting Started with Local Organizing
articles.body: You don't need to be an expert to start organizing in your community. This guide walks through basic steps like mapping your community, hosting a meeting, and planning your first campaign.
articles.summary: A beginner-friendly guide to local community organizing.

articles.title: Youth Perspectives on Police Reform in Schools
articles.body:
Young people are leading efforts to remove police from schools and invest in counselors, nurses, and restorative justice. In this article, students share their experiences and visions for safer schools.

Their stories highlight how school-based policing contributes to the school-to-prison pipeline and why youth-led organizing is essential.
articles.summary: Students share their experiences and demands for police reform in schools.

events.title: Midtown NYC Voting Rights Voter Registration Drive
events.description: Join volunteers in Midtown Manhattan to help community members register to vote, check their registration status, and learn about their voting rights.
events.accessibility_notes: Wheelchair accessible entrance and restrooms; ASL interpretation available upon request.

events.title: Brooklyn Voting Rights Community Forum
events.description: A bilingual community forum on voter suppression, ballot access, and upcoming elections in New York.
events.accessibility_notes: Ramp access and priority seating are available; live CART captioning will be provided.

events.title: Hoboken Poll Monitor Training
events.description: Learn how to serve as a nonpartisan poll monitor and protect voting rights on Election Day.
events.accessibility_notes: Elevator access to all floors; please email for additional accessibility needs.

events.title: National Voting Rights Online Town Hall
events.description: A virtual town hall with organizers from across the country discussing current threats to voting rights and opportunities to take action.
events.accessibility_notes: Live captions provided; recording available for registered participants.

events.title: San Francisco Climate Justice Rally
events.description: Gather with frontline communities and allies to demand bold local climate action and investments in clean energy.
events.accessibility_notes: Outdoor event with level grounds; ASL interpretation provided.

events.title: Mission District Neighborhood Climate Cleanup
events.description: Help clean up local streets, distribute climate justice flyers, and talk with neighbors about upcoming campaigns.
events.accessibility_notes: Some uneven terrain on grass; accessible restrooms located nearby.

events.title: Los Angeles Police Accountability Town Hall
events.description: Community members, legal experts, and local officials discuss police oversight and reform proposals.
events.accessibility_notes: ADA-compliant facility; Spanish and English interpretation provided.

events.title: Virtual Town Hall on Refugee Rights
events.description: Hear from refugees, advocates, and legal experts about current threats to refugee protection and how to respond.
events.accessibility_notes: Live captions and Spanish interpretation will be available.

events.title: Student Debt Cancellation Strategy Call
events.description: Organizers and borrowers discuss upcoming opportunities to push for federal and state-level student debt relief.
events.accessibility_notes: Closed captions and transcript provided after the call.

events.title: San Francisco Tenant Rights Clinic
events.description: Free legal clinic for renters facing eviction, rent hikes, or unsafe housing conditions.
events.accessibility_notes: Elevator access to second floor; please avoid strong fragrances.

events.title: Economic Justice and Recovery Online Forum
events.description: A conversation about living wages, public investment, and just recovery from economic crises.

events.title: Grassroots Organizing Skills Training
events.description: Learn the fundamentals of base-building, storytelling, and campaign planning.
events.accessibility_notes: Wheelchair accessible; childcare and light snacks provided.

forum_categories.name: Immigration & Refugee Rights
forum_categories.description: Discuss immigration policy, detention, deportation defense, refugee resettlement, and solidarity with migrant communities.

forum_categories.name: Climate & Environment
forum_categories.description: Share strategies for climate action, clean energy, local environmental justice fights, and global climate policy.

forum_categories.name: Climate Justice
forum_categories.description: Center frontline communities and intersectional approaches to climate justice organizing.

forum_categories.name: Criminal Justice & Policing
forum_categories.description: Talk about police reform, prison abolition, decarceration, and community-led safety models.

forum_categories.name: Housing & Homelessness
forum_categories.description: Connect with others organizing for rent control, tenant protections, and an end to homelessness.

forum_categories.name: Voting Rights & Elections
forum_categories.description: Plan voter registration drives, fight voter suppression, and share election-related resources.

message_templates.title: Support Affordable Housing Legislation
message_templates.default_subject: Please Support Strong Affordable Housing Legislation
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

I am a constituent in your district, and I am writing to urge you to support strong affordable housing legislation. Far too many families in our community are struggling to keep a roof over their heads.

Comprehensive affordable housing policy should include robust funding for deeply affordable units, protections against unjust evictions, and support for non-profit and community-owned housing models.

Please champion policies that ensure everyone in our district has access to safe, stable, and affordable housing.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Address]

message_templates.title: Extend and Strengthen Eviction Protections
message_templates.default_subject: Protect Renters by Extending Eviction Protections
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

I am asking you to extend and strengthen eviction protections to prevent a surge in homelessness. Many renters are still recovering financially and cannot afford displacement.

Please support policies that provide rental assistance, require just cause for evictions, and expand legal support for tenants.

Our community is stronger when everyone has a stable home.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Invest in Renewable Energy and Green Jobs
message_templates.default_subject: Support Bold Investments in Renewable Energy
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

Climate change is a defining challenge of our time, and we need bold action now. I urge you to support major investments in renewable energy, energy efficiency, and green jobs in our community.

These investments will reduce climate pollution, lower energy bills, and create good-paying jobs.

Please prioritize policies that rapidly transition us away from fossil fuels and toward a clean energy future.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Center Frontline Communities in Climate Policy
message_templates.default_subject: Climate Policy Must Center Frontline Communities
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

Frontline communities are bearing the brunt of pollution and climate impacts. Climate policy must center their needs and leadership.

Please support legislation that directs climate investments to communities most harmed, strengthens environmental justice protections, and ensures community participation in decision-making.

We have an opportunity to build a more just and livable future for everyone.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Protect and Expand Voting Rights
message_templates.default_subject: Defend Voting Rights and Expand Ballot Access
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

I am deeply concerned about ongoing efforts to restrict voting rights. Our democracy works best when every eligible voter can cast a ballot that counts.

Please support legislation that expands early voting, protects vote-by-mail, restores rights to people with past convictions, and prevents voter suppression.

I urge you to be a vocal champion for free and fair elections.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Advance Police Accountability and Community Safety
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

I am writing to ask you to support strong police accountability and community safety reforms. Our community needs effective oversight, transparency, and alternatives to over-policing.

Please back policies such as independent investigations of police misconduct, public reporting of use-of-force data, and investment in non-police crisis response teams.

Everyone deserves to feel safe and respected in their community.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Support Student Debt Relief
message_templates.default_subject: Provide Relief for Student Loan Borrowers
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

Student debt is holding back millions of people from building stable, thriving lives. I urge you to support meaningful student debt relief.

Please champion policies that reduce or cancel federal and state student loan balances, cap monthly payments based on income, and invest in debt-free public higher education moving forward.

Education should be a pathway to opportunity, not a lifetime burden.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Create a Pathway to Citizenship
message_templates.default_subject: Support a Fair Pathway to Citizenship
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

I urge you to support legislation that creates a fair, accessible pathway to citizenship for undocumented immigrants and long-term residents.

Our communities are stronger when families can live without fear of deportation and fully participate in civic life.

Please oppose policies that criminalize migration and instead invest in humane, rights-respecting immigration systems.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Raise the Minimum Wage
message_templates.default_subject: Support a Living Wage for All Workers
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

Working people deserve wages that keep up with the cost of living. I urge you to support raising the minimum wage to a true living wage.

Higher wages reduce poverty, strengthen local economies, and help families afford basic needs.

Please stand with workers and support a strong minimum wage increase.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

message_templates.title: Share Your General Concerns with Your Representative
message_templates.default_body:
Dear [Representative Name],

As your constituent, I want to share my concerns about several issues affecting our community.

[Insert your specific concerns here.]

I hope you will take these priorities into account as you make decisions on upcoming legislation.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

petitions.title: End Cash Bail in Our County
petitions.description:
Cash bail forces people to stay in jail before trial simply because they cannot afford to pay. This system punishes poverty and disproportionately harms Black and Brown communities.

We call on our county officials to end the use of cash bail, invest in pretrial services, and ensure that freedom before trial does not depend on wealth.

petitions.title: Protect the Right to Vote for All
petitions.description:
Voter suppression tactics continue to block people—especially voters of color, young people, and low-income communities—from accessing the ballot.

We urge state legislators to pass comprehensive voting rights protections, expand early voting, and ensure that every eligible voter can cast a ballot that counts.

petitions.title: Relief for Borrowers Crushed by Student Debt
petitions.description:
Student debt keeps millions of borrowers from buying homes, starting families, or saving for the future. The burden is especially heavy for low-income borrowers and borrowers of color.

We call on federal and state policymakers to enact robust student debt relief and invest in debt-free public higher education.

petitions.title: Adopt a 100% Renewable Energy Standard
petitions.description:
Our state must commit to rapid decarbonization by setting a binding target for 100% renewable electricity.

We urge lawmakers to pass a strong renewable portfolio standard with clear timelines, labor protections, and investments in frontline communities.

petitions.title: Expand Rent Control and Tenant Protections
petitions.description:
Rents are rising faster than wages, pushing long-time residents out of their neighborhoods.

We demand expanded rent control, stronger tenant protections against harassment and unjust evictions, and investments in truly affordable housing.

petitions.title: End Immigration Detention in Our State
petitions.description:
Private immigration detention centers profit from locking up families and individuals seeking safety.

We call on state leaders to end contracts with private detention companies and invest in community-based support instead.

petitions.title: Establish a Citywide Living Wage Standard
petitions.description:
Every worker deserves a wage that covers basic needs like housing, food, and healthcare.

We urge city leaders to adopt a living wage ordinance that lifts wages and protects workers from exploitation.

petitions.title: Restore Service on the 15 Bus Route
petitions.description:
Recent cuts to the 15 bus route have left many residents without reliable transportation to work, school, and medical appointments.

We call on transit officials to restore frequent, accessible service on this critical line.

threads.title: What would a fair pathway to citizenship actually look like?
threads.body:
I’ve been calling my reps using the “fair pathway to citizenship” script, but I want to understand more concretely what we should be pushing for. For those organizing on immigration reform, what are the key policy demands you’re centering right now (e.g., length of residency requirement, fees, criminal bars, work authorization during the process)?

If you have examples of state or federal bills that get this mostly right (or very wrong), please share so we can reference specific language when we contact officials.

threads.title: Best ways to support asylum seekers in our city right now?
threads.body:
Our mutual aid group has been getting more requests from asylum-seeking families who just arrived and are navigating the system. Beyond donations, what concrete support have people found most helpful—court accompaniment, childcare, translation, rapid response networks?

Also, how are you all pushing local officials to fund community-based support instead of detention and surveillance?

threads.title: Strategies for closing private immigration detention centers
threads.body:
Our state still has contracts with two private detention companies. We’re circulating the petition to end immigration detention, but we’re trying to map out the next steps.

What campaigns have successfully shut down detention centers in your state? Did you target the governor, legislature, county sheriffs, or corporations first? Any sample resolutions, talking points, or media strategies would be really helpful.

threads.title: Has anyone set up a "welcome team" for newly arrived refugees?
threads.body:
Our congregation wants to organize a small "welcome team" for newly arrived refugee families—helping with transportation, school enrollment, and basic navigation of the city.

If you’ve done this, what did you wish you’d known at the beginning? Any do’s and don’ts to avoid putting extra pressure on families or crossing boundaries?

threads.title: DACA and undocumented students: campus organizing ideas?
threads.body:
Our campus immigrant rights group is planning a week of action around DACA and undocumented student protections. We’re thinking about a call-in day to state reps, a teach-in, and maybe a storytelling event.

If your campus has run similar campaigns, what tactics actually moved administrators or local decision-makers? Any tips for protecting students’ privacy while still making a strong public case?

threads.title: Tips for organizing a neighborhood climate cleanup
threads.body: We’re planning a cleanup in our neighborhood similar to the Mission District event listed in the Events section. For folks who’ve done this before, what helped you get strong turnout and tie the cleanup to broader climate campaigns (like renewable energy or transit funding) instead of it just being a one-off volunteer day?

threads.title: Pushing our city to commit to 100% renewable electricity
threads.body:
After reading the renewable energy explainer and the petition about a 100% renewable standard, our local group wants to push our city to make a similar commitment.

Has anyone here worked on a local 100% renewable campaign? How did you frame the demand, and who were the key decision-makers you had to win over (mayor, utility board, etc.)?

threads.title: Ensuring climate funds actually reach frontline communities
threads.body:
Our state just passed a major climate bill, but we’re worried the funding won’t truly reach frontline communities. The climate justice article talks about this, but I’d love concrete examples.

How are you all tracking where climate dollars go, and what language have you successfully pushed for to guarantee investments in frontline neighborhoods?

threads.title: Coordinating for the upcoming San Francisco climate rally
threads.body:
Who here is going to the San Francisco Climate Justice Rally listed on the Events page? Our group from the Mission is planning to meet beforehand and bring banners about transit and housing links to climate justice.

If you’re organizing a contingent (youth, labor, faith, etc.), share meeting points and messages so we can show up in a coordinated way.

threads.title: Alternatives to police-led traffic enforcement in our city
threads.body:
After reading the "Rethinking Traffic Stops" article, our coalition wants to propose an unarmed traffic safety team. For those who have tried this, what pushback did you get from police unions or city officials, and how did you respond?

Any sample policy language or pilot program designs you can share would help us build our proposal.

threads.title: Removing police from schools: advice from youth organizers?
threads.body:
We’re organizing students and parents to remove police from our district’s schools and invest in counselors, nurses, and restorative justice instead. The youth perspectives article really resonated with our group.

If you’ve won or are fighting for similar changes, what were the most effective tactics—school board testimony, walkouts, student petitions, or something else?

threads.title: Coordinating against rent hikes in the 94110 zip code
threads.body:
Several buildings in 94110 just got steep rent increases all at once. We’re trying to connect tenants across buildings to push back collectively and possibly support the rent control petition.

If you’re a renter in 94110 or nearby, reply here or share how your tenant association has negotiated with landlords or used local rent boards to fight hikes.

threads.title: How student debt and rising rents are colliding
threads.body:
Many of us are paying huge chunks of our income to both landlords and loan servicers. After reading the student debt article and the rent burden explainer, I’m curious how groups are framing these issues together.

Has anyone done joint campaigns or storytelling projects that connect student debt cancellation to affordable housing demands?

threads.title: Who’s going to the Midtown NYC voter registration drive?
threads.body:
The Events page lists a Midtown NYC Voting Rights Voter Registration Drive near zip 10001. Our small group is planning to attend and could use more volunteers to help with registration and language support.

If you’re in or near 10001 and can come, what times work for you, and what training do you feel you need beforehand?

threads.title: Questions before signing up for poll monitor training
threads.body:
I’m thinking about attending the Hoboken poll monitor training listed on the site but I’m nervous about what to expect. How much legal knowledge do you need beforehand, and what do poll monitors actually do on Election Day?

If you’ve been a poll monitor, please share what surprised you most and any tips you wish you’d known.

comments.body: For our coalition, a fair pathway to citizenship has three core pieces: a clear and reasonable timeline (no more than 8 years), work authorization from day one, and fees that are scaled to income. We also push hard against broad criminal bars because they end up re-punishing people who have already served their sentences.

Co-signing this. We also try to spell out automatic green cards for long-term residents with deep community ties, not just temporary protections. One practical tip: when you talk to state reps, connect federal pathway demands to state-level wins like access to licenses, in-state tuition, and legal defense funds.

Weve been using the New Way Forward Act and some state-level legalization bills as reference points. They are not perfect, but they have strong language around ending mandatory detention and reducing the role of criminalization in immigration law. When we call, we name specific bill sections instead of speaking in generalities.

This is really helpful. We adapted the bill language into a one-page "principles for a real pathway" handout. It lists what we want on timelines, detentions, and access to public benefits after legalization. Staffers keep that sheet on their desks, which makes follow-up meetings way easier.

One thing Id add is protections during the process itself. People need protection from deportation while their applications are pending, plus access to healthcare and work. Otherwise, the pathway looks good on paper but is unlivable in real life, especially for low-wage workers and families with kids.

Agree. We had a case locally where someone lost work authorization mid-process and almost lost housing. Now we always highlight automatic renewal of work permits and drivers licenses as non-negotiable demands.

Court accompaniment and transportation stipends have been huge for our group. People are terrified walking into immigration court alone, and having a team there changes the tone. We also set up a rotating childcare crew during legal appointments, which made it easier for parents to actually show up.

Seconding this and adding one thing: build in time for political education, not just services. We do monthly know-your-rights sessions that connect peoples individual cases to the bigger fight to end detention and expand asylum protections. Thats where a lot of new leaders emerge.

In our state, we started by mapping the money: who owns the detention centers, who gets the contracts, and which county officials sign off. Then we ran a two-track strategy: one aimed at the governor for a state contract ban, and one at county sheriffs to stop new agreements. Local media pressure was key.

We learned to ask families what they actually want instead of assuming. Some want help navigating schools and transit, others really just want a calm friend who can explain mail and paperwork. We also created a simple boundaries sheet so volunteers know when to slow down and respect privacy.

For our campus, the most effective tactic was a coordinated student walkout paired with a packed public hearing at the board of trustees. We had faculty ready to back students up and lawyers on hand to advise about risks. Storytelling from undocumented students was powerful but strictly voluntary and anonymized when needed.

Love this model. Were thinking about a similar walkout but want to make sure students dont face retaliation. Did you negotiate anything with administrators ahead of time, like amnesty from attendance penalties?

We paired our cleanup with a quick teach-in at the beginning and a petition action at the end. People picked up trash for an hour, then signed postcards demanding a 100% renewable commitment from the city. It helped folks see the cleanup as part of a bigger strategy instead of just a feel-good event.

Our labor contingent is meeting near the Civic Center BART entrance at 11:30. Well have signs connecting climate justice to worker safety and public transit funding. If anyone from housing groups wants to link up, we would love shared chants around rent control and green, affordable housing.

When we proposed an unarmed traffic team, police unions argued it would hurt "officer safety." We countered with data from cities that already use civilian responders and showed reductions in use-of-force incidents. Getting local businesses and disability advocates to testify together really shifted a few skeptical council members.

Tenants in two buildings on my block just formed associations after 25% rent hikes. We filed complaints with the rent board and coordinated a joint letter from all affected tenants. Having one shared demand letter, instead of everyone negotiating alone, made the landlord take us a lot more seriously.

I can volunteer for the first two hours of the Midtown drive. Ive done registration before but would love a quick refresher on New Yorks rules around party affiliation changes and deadlines. If someone has a simple script or checklist we can use at the table, please drop it here.

Ive been a poll monitor for three elections. You dont need to be a legal expertthe training walks you through common problems like wrong ID requests or people missing from the rolls. The biggest thing is staying calm, documenting everything, and calling the hotline when something feels off.

campaigns.title: Win a 100% Local Renewable Energy Commitment
campaigns.short_description: Push your city to adopt a binding 100% renewable electricity standard with strong equity provisions.
campaigns.full_description:
This campaign is organizing residents, workers, and frontline communities to demand that our city commit to 100% renewable electricity on a rapid timeline. We’re building a broad coalition that includes environmental justice groups, labor unions, and renters facing high energy bills.

Volunteers help gather petition signatures, attend city council meetings, and share stories about how high utility bills and pollution impact their neighborhoods. Together, we can move our local grid off fossil fuels and ensure that clean energy investments benefit those most affected by climate change.

campaigns.title: Upgrade Buildings with Bold Energy Efficiency Standards
campaigns.short_description: Fight for stronger building codes and retrofit programs that cut emissions and utility bills.
campaigns.full_description:
Buildings are one of the largest sources of climate pollution in many cities. This campaign pushes for strong energy-efficiency standards, incentives for climate-friendly retrofits, and protections so that low-income renters share in the benefits.

Supporters help collect stories from tenants dealing with drafty, unhealthy housing, lobby local officials for new building performance standards, and educate neighbors about available retrofit programs. By tightening standards and funding upgrades, we can cut emissions while keeping homes healthier and more affordable.

campaigns.title: Fund Frontline Climate Justice Communities
campaigns.short_description: Redirect climate dollars to neighborhoods facing the greatest pollution and climate risk.
campaigns.full_description:
Frontline communities are already dealing with extreme heat, flooding, and toxic air. This campaign demands that new climate funding prioritize those neighborhoods first. We’re tracking where climate dollars actually go and pushing for community-led oversight.

Participants help analyze public budget data, support local climate justice groups to testify at hearings, and use story-based media to highlight community-led solutions. Your support ensures that climate policy doesn’t leave the most impacted people behind.

campaigns.title: Launch Community Solar for Low-Income Renters
campaigns.short_description: Create a community solar program so renters can access clean energy savings.
campaigns.full_description:
Most rooftop solar programs leave renters out. This campaign aims to create a community solar program that lets low-income renters subscribe to local solar projects and see guaranteed savings on their bills.

Volunteers survey tenants about energy burdens, advocate for fair subscription rules at utility commission hearings, and help design outreach materials in multiple languages. Community solar can democratize clean energy—if we design it with equity at the center.

campaigns.title: Win Green Jobs for Youth in Frontline Neighborhoods
campaigns.short_description: Secure funding for youth apprenticeships in renewable energy, transit, and climate resilience.
campaigns.full_description:
Young people in frontline communities need good, stable jobs that also help build a livable future. This campaign pushes for public investment in paid apprenticeships and training programs for youth in renewable energy, transit, and climate resilience projects.

Supporters help craft policy proposals, share testimony at hearings, and mobilize youth turnout at climate justice rallies. Winning green jobs for youth is key to a just transition away from fossil fuels.

campaigns.title: Raise the State Minimum Wage to a Living Wage
campaigns.short_description: Organize workers and voters to win a higher state minimum wage indexed to the cost of living.
campaigns.full_description:
Workers across the state are demanding a minimum wage that actually covers rent, food, and healthcare. This campaign combines workplace organizing with public pressure on legislators to pass a strong, indexed minimum wage law.

Volunteers talk with coworkers, share stories at town halls, phone-bank voters in key districts, and meet with state representatives. By raising the floor for the lowest-paid workers, we can reduce poverty and narrow the racial wealth gap.

campaigns.title: Launch a Guaranteed Income Pilot in Our City
campaigns.short_description: Win a publicly funded guaranteed income pilot for low-income residents.
campaigns.full_description:
Guaranteed income pilots have shown that direct cash with no strings attached helps families stabilize their lives and plan for the future. This campaign calls on local officials to launch a guaranteed income pilot that prioritizes low-wage workers, caregivers, and undocumented residents left out of other programs.

Supporters help gather stories about the impact of economic insecurity, advocate at budget hearings, and build a coalition with faith groups and small businesses. Together, we can prove that economic justice means trusting people with the resources they need.

campaigns.title: Cancel Student Debt for Low-Income Borrowers
campaigns.short_description: Organize borrowers to demand broad student debt cancellation and stronger income-based repayment.
campaigns.full_description:
Student debt is trapping an entire generation in financial precarity. This campaign centers low-income borrowers and borrowers of color who carry the heaviest burdens. We’re fighting for broad cancellation of federal and state student loans and for robust protections against predatory servicers.

Participants share their stories, pressure lawmakers with coordinated call-in days, and support petitions calling for debt cancellation and debt-free public higher education. By acting together, we can transform education from a debt sentence into a public good.

campaigns.title: Make Community College Tuition-Free
campaigns.short_description: Push state leaders to guarantee tuition-free community college for all residents.
campaigns.full_description:
Community colleges are vital gateways to opportunity, but tuition and fees still keep many students out. This campaign advocates for state legislation that makes community college tuition-free, with additional support for books, childcare, and transportation.

Supporters gather student stories, meet with education committee members, and turn out for rallies at the state capitol. Winning tuition-free community college is a major step toward a truly equitable education system.

campaigns.title: Win Real Transparency in Policing Data
campaigns.short_description: Demand public reporting of stops, use of force, and complaints to advance police accountability.
campaigns.full_description:
Without accurate data, it’s impossible to hold police departments accountable. This campaign calls for strong transparency laws requiring public reporting of stops, searches, use-of-force incidents, and misconduct complaints, broken down by race, gender, and neighborhood.

Volunteers analyze existing data, support impacted residents in sharing their stories, and lobby city and state officials to pass robust transparency measures. Making policing data public is a critical step toward meaningful reform and community-led safety.

campaigns.title: Create a Strong Civilian Police Oversight Board
campaigns.short_description: Establish an independent civilian board with real power over police policy and discipline.
campaigns.full_description:
Communities deserve a real say in how they are policed. This campaign demands the creation—or strengthening—of a civilian oversight board with independent investigative power, subpoena authority, and meaningful control over discipline and policy.

Supporters help draft charter language, educate neighbors about what strong oversight looks like, and turn out for key city council and ballot measure votes. A strong oversight board can help shift power from police departments to the people they serve.

campaigns.title: End Cash Bail and Invest in Pretrial Justice
campaigns.short_description: Abolish money bail and replace it with fair, evidence-based pretrial systems.
campaigns.full_description:
Cash bail keeps people in jail before trial simply because they are poor, while wealthier people buy their freedom. This campaign fights to end cash bail locally and statewide, replacing it with fair, evidence-based pretrial practices that respect due process.

Participants support impacted families, push for legislation reflecting the demands in the “End Cash Bail in Our County” petition, and challenge fear-based narratives from bail lobbyists. Ending cash bail is essential to shrinking jail populations and advancing racial and economic justice.

campaigns.title: Pass Strong Federal Voting Rights and Fair Maps
campaigns.short_description: Support federal legislation to protect voting rights and end partisan gerrymandering.
campaigns.full_description:
Voter suppression and gerrymandering undermine democracy at every level. This campaign mobilizes constituents to support robust federal voting rights and fair maps legislation that expands access, restores protections, and curbs partisan map-rigging.

Supporters call and email members of Congress, share personal stories about barriers to voting, and help lead local teach-ins using materials from our voting rights resources. Federal action is critical to defending democracy everywhere.

campaigns.title: Expand Language Access for NYC Voters
campaigns.short_description: Ensure ballots, materials, and poll sites serve voters in all major languages.
campaigns.full_description:
Many New Yorkers face language barriers when they go to vote. This campaign advocates for expanded translation of ballots and voter materials, more bilingual poll workers, and better outreach to communities that vote in languages other than English.

Volunteers collect stories at voter registration drives like the Midtown NYC event, document language access problems on Election Day, and push city and state officials to strengthen protections. Language access is essential for a truly inclusive democracy.

campaigns.title: Strengthen Rent Control and Tenant Protections in 94110
campaigns.short_description: Organize Mission District tenants against rent hikes and displacement.
campaigns.full_description:
Tenants in zip code 94110 are facing steep rent hikes and displacement. This campaign brings together renters across buildings to strengthen local rent control, win just-cause eviction protections, and support the citywide petition for stronger tenant rights.

Supporters help form tenant associations, share information about rent board hearings, and contact representatives using our housing message templates to demand bold action on affordability. When tenants act together, landlords can’t play them off against each other.

campaigns.title: End Immigration Detention Contracts in Our State
campaigns.short_description: Shut down private immigration detention centers and invest in community-based support.
campaigns.full_description:
Private immigration detention centers profit from locking up people seeking safety. This campaign pushes state officials to end contracts with private detention companies, limit local collaboration with ICE, and fund community-based support instead.

Volunteers support local organizing near detention centers, amplify stories from detained people and their families, and lobby governors and legislators to pass detention abolition bills. We connect directly to the petition to end immigration detention and coordinate strategies with groups that have already won shutdowns in their states.
